name: S&P 500
summary: Day 68 Wednesday — **S&P 500 7,259.22 (+0.8% combined Tue +0.55% recovering Mon -0.41%); NASDAQ 25,326.13 (+1.0%, +15.1% 1M); DOW 49,298.25 (+0.7%); VIX 17.38 (-5.0%, -28.1% 1M, BELOW PRE-MAY 4 BASELINE); SMH $522.69 (+3.1%, +32.0% 1M); KOSPI 7,400.32 (+6.7% RECORD; SAMSUNG $1T MKT CAP); LOCKHEED $508.93 (-1.8%, -20.2% 1M cumulative defense de-rating).** The cleanest cross-asset risk-on regime of the entire 67-day arc. Trump's May 5 pause of Project Freedom triggered: VIX collapse below pre-May 4 baseline (full risk-off impulse unwound in 36 hours); Russell 2000 +~2% (small-caps lead in pause-pricing); DAX +1.7%; KOSPI +6.7% to record on Samsung $1T market cap (Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production talks → AI-cycle decoupling structurally validated). The May 5-6 sequence is the cleanest validation that the AI capex cycle has structurally decoupled from West Asia geopolitical risk for major Asian (Korea, Taiwan) AND US tech exporters. **CRITICAL DISAMBIGUATION: today's metals rally (gold +2.7%, silver +4.5%, copper +2.6%) is DOLLAR-DRIVEN, NOT war-risk bid** — DXY -0.4% on Trump-comfortable-with-weak-dollar comments + BlackRock Rieder dovish-Fed-chair speculation. Cross-asset confirmation that this is dollar story not war story: VIX DOWN -5%, defense ITA stays weak, S&P UP, yields LOWER. Lockheed's Q1 EPS miss ($6.44 vs $7.28 prior year) + supply chain delays = ~50% earnings, ~50% crisis-de-rating; the 'defense as a crisis hedge' trade has structurally failed — AI/semiconductor rotation has been the actual hedge. The 'VIX 22+ trigger (long-vol entry signal)' = NOT activated; with VIX at 17.38 the trade has unwound to baseline. Goldman Q2 Brent cut to $90 (from $99) reinforces equity de-risk-premium. CME June cut probability ~28%; Polymarket ~56% zero 2026 cuts.
